caption: DIC time-lapse images of wild-type, mei-1(or642 ts) and mei-1(or646 ts) embryos. The two mei-1(or642 ts) embryos were obtained from a hermaphrodite shifted to the restrictive temperature for 30 minutes, the mei-1(or646 ts) embryo was obtained from a hermaphrodite shifted to the restrictive temperature for 7 hours prior to imaging. White arrowheads indicates polar bodies, black arrowheads indicate multiple maternal pronuclei, the black arrow denotes multiple nuclei per cell at the two cell stage, and the “p” refers to the paternal pronucleus in an embryo lacking a maternal pronucleus. Times in min:sec are given relative to nuclear envelope breakdown (NEBD). Scale bar, 10 µm

caption: Scanning-laser ophthalmoscopy (SLO) imaging. Eyes from Rosa26 (wt; upper row), Rosa26-floxSTOP/SRF-VP16 (het) (middle row), and Rosa26-floxSTOP/SRF-VP16 (hom; bottom row) animals at six months of age (P180) were investigated. Fundus imaging with RF at 514 nm excitation (left column) and autofluorescence at 488 nm laser wavelengths (middle left column) display dramatic changes in fundus appearance, structure of the nerve fiber layer and accumulation of autofluorescent lipids in genetically modified animals compared to wild-type animals. Retinal degenerative processes can also be inferred from enhanced angiographic deep layer (choroidal) signals using fluorescein (middle right column) and indocyanine green (right column) angiography, as clearly detected in both heterozygous and homozygous animals.
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is Scanning-laser ophthalmoscopy (SLO).
A2: This image depicts the eyes and specifically focuses on areas like fundus, nerve fiber layer and the retinal layers.

A3: Yes, there are abnormalities identified in this image. These include dramatic changes in fundus appearance, structure of the nerve fiber layer, accumulation of autofluorescent lipids, and retinal degenerative processes in genetically modified animals.

A4: The image does show irregularities such as dramatic changes in fundus appearance and structure of the nerve fiber layer, accumulation of autofluorescent lipids, and enhanced angiographic deep layer signals indicative of retinal degenerative processes. These were clearly detected in both heterozygous and homozygous animals.

A5: The image contains labels or indices that are noteworthy such as illustrations of the effects of genetic modifications in Rosa26-floxSTOP/SRF-VP16 (het) and Rosa26-floxSTOP/SRF-VP16 (hom) animals in comparison to wild-type animals. caption: Characterization of Hb following preterm rabbit pup IVH. The presence of Hb following IVH was characterized within the brain utilizing the inherent peroxidase activity of Hb. Rabbit pups with IVH or sham controls were euthanized at 72 h of age followed by saline and freshly prepared 4% PFA perfusion. Afterwards the brains were dissected out from the skulls and immersed in 4% PFA. Brains were prepared and sections and a number of subventricular and periventricular anatomically comparable regions of interests (ROI) were chosen. Neuroanatomically defined ROI's located in the rostral forebrain at the levels of rostral forebrain (Level 1), caudal forebrain (Level 2), rostral midbrain (Level 3) and caudal midbrain (Level 4), were stained for peroxidase activity of Hb as described in the Materials and Methods Section. Microscope analyses were performed on a wide-field Olympus microscope (IX73) and slide scanning were performed on a Hamamatsu NanoZoomer 2.0-HT Digital slide scanner: C10730. Scanning was performed with a 40 × magnification lens. Images used for illustrations, from regions of interest (ROI), were grabbed with the viewer software NDP.view2 Viewing software. Scale bar of slide scan image indicate 2.5 mm and of ROI images indicate 500 μm.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not explicitly stated in the caption, but it mentions the use of an Olympus microscope and a digital slide scanner, suggesting microscopy images.
A2: The image depicts the brain of preterm rabbit pups. Specific regions of interest include the rostral forebrain, caudal forebrain, rostral midbrain, and caudal midbrain.
A3: The caption does not mention any abnormalities identified in the image.
A4: The caption does not provide information to determine if the image appears normal or if it shows any irregularities.

A5: The image includes rostral forebrain (Level 1), caudal forebrain (Level 2), rostral midbrain (Level 3) and caudal midbrain (Level 4) as neuroanatomically defined regions of interest (ROI). It also uses a scale bar of the slide scan image (2.5 mm) and ROI image (500 μm) as noteworthy indices.
